text: What's up guys, you are back in 100 days of code and today I will tell you about function arguments that how can arguments be passed to function we said that function is used to separate code but there are many ways by which we can pass arguments to function we can pass some arguments to function as a tuple and we can pass as a dictionary let's go to the computer screen and see how default arguments are passed many arguments which you want to pass in bulk how are they passed let's get started Code Separation we all know that function is used to separate code and we can repeat it as many times as we want today I will create a function and I will name it as average and I will print and I will print the average is and I will write here a plus b by 2 now you have to remember one thing that if you are doing calculation like this then you should know that which calculation you want to do first in this case if I show you I want to do a plus b by 2 first and after that I want to do this by 2 so I will run this program so you can see here nothing happened why nothing happened because I have given two numbers which I have to calculate the average and obviously I will do a comma b and now I will run as you can see the average is 5.0 it is printed here so this is a simple function there are four types of arguments that we can provide in a function default arguments, keyword arguments variable length arguments and required arguments in this case a and b are required arguments now let's talk about default arguments I can give a value 9 and b value 1 by default in this case if I run the average I will run the average like this then it will work and it will assume a value as 9 and b value as 1 and give me 5 but if I give a value as 1 and b value as 5 then it will ignore 9 and 1 and it will take a and b value respectively so my answer will be 3 as you can see so these are our default arguments we can provide a default value while creating a function this way the function assumes a default value even if a value is not provided in the function for that argument now here I want to give a value and want to put b value as default so what I will do here let's say I give 4 or 5 and I run it so you can see I gave a value as 5 and I didn't give b value so b value will be 1 and 5 plus 1 by 2 6 by 2 means 3 will be printed if I want to give b value then I will write something like this let's say b is equal to 9 a value is 9 b value will be 9 and 9.0 will be printed so I can give any value like this and the other value will be taken from the default arguments so these are the default arguments now here I have given an example that a function named name is made first name, middle name, last name and after that if you give only first name then your middle name and last name will be from the default arguments if you give all three then obviously your first name, middle name and last name will be from the default arguments and I want to run this example and see here when we are printing hello amy john watson is written if I write here amy agarwal then amy agarwal watson will be printed you can see amy agarwal watson if I print amy agarwal jain then amy agarwal jain will be printed so we have seen that amy agarwal jain is printed now what we will do I will remove this example because it is already given in rappel now let's talk about keyword arguments keyword arguments are very simple arguments which you can give like this you can change the order of arguments like in this case if I want to give value of b then I can give value of b as 9 and a is equal to 21 and if a is equal to 21 then b is equal to 9 so I don't need to give in that order so this is how they are used we can provide arguments with key is equal to value this way the interpreter recognizes the arguments by the parameter name hence the order in which the arguments are passed does not matter if you want that order doesn't matter if you give arguments by default then you have to give a first if you use this kind of syntax then you don't have to care about the order so here this thing is also clear now see what are required arguments required arguments are those arguments which you have to give now if I don't write a is equal to 9 then a will become required argument you have to give value of a you can't escape from that so you have to give value of a whether you give value of b or not if you give then it will take that value if you don't give then it will take the default value of b simple thing in case we don't pass the arguments it is necessary to pass the arguments in the correct position order and the number of arguments passed should match with actual function definition this is very important if here value of a, b and c are taken suppose 3 values are taken c is also taken so I write a, b and c is equal to 1 so c is optional if you are giving a or b without key is equal to value then you have to give like this suppose 5, 6 so value of a is 5 and value of b is 6 and here I will comment this function because I have to give 3 values so average is 6.0 value of a is 5, value of b is 6 and default value of c is taken so this is how you can do now you have to run this and tell me whether you could do it successfully or not, whether you understood things or not now let's see about variable length argument so what happens is you can give variable length argument like suppose I have to get average of many numbers now I can get average of 3 numbers, 4 numbers I can get average of 10 numbers so what I will do I will do something like this I will do numbers I will comment this and I will write it again I will comment this also and I will write this function again so I will write here star numbers now numbers is an iterable for i in numbers and I will write sum is equal to sum plus i and finally what I will do that I will write print average is and I will write here something like this average is sum divided by length of numbers and I will initialize sum from 0 something like this and finally if I will do average 5, 6 so it is giving me 5.5 so what happened is it took numbers as a tuple if I print here type of numbers so I will get tuple so it will take as a tuple so it will take tuple so I hope you are getting it how it is working behind the scenes what is happening is I am getting a tuple of numbers means I am getting a list of numbers so we will see in the future what is the difference in tuple list and how we can use it but for now you can understand that we have looped on it and we have added all the numbers and divided all the numbers from them what we got by doing this we are able to get average now I can get 5, 6 or 5, 6, 7 or 15 or 25 no problem now 6 is here if I do this so the average will change sum divided by length in this case it is 4 so it will be divided by 4 so I hope you understood now assume that I want to take as a dictionary my arguments so what I can do I can use this kind of syntax I will copy it and show you and I am commenting the average so there is a function named name if I will write star star name so whatever key is equal to value I will give it I will get it as a last name you got it like this so what you are doing here you are taking the name as a dictionary so if I will type print type of name so it will be printed as a dictionary so I will indent it properly in fact I did it a little more but that's fine so you can see here what is happening indentation error so class dict is printed here so why class dict is printed because it is taking as a will teach you in the chapter of dictionary so we will see it that day for now you can ignore it now come back to my average function now what happens when I am running the function so I want function to return a value instead of printing the average I want it to return so I will write return here and you keep watching as soon as I will write return here and I will write here c is equal to this and I will print c so I will get this value here so basically what is this average it is computing average of all numbers and it is storing it in c so what happens by return so when you return it will be stored here so in calling function there you will get the value which you want finally what you want from average, you want value so now if I run it so you will see that average of these numbers will come in c so you will get 4.75 if I don't return this so you will get none because this function is not returning so you will see none but I want to return this value so if I return 7 so what will I get, obviously 7 if we put two return statements so first return is accepted and second return and all the returns you put after that are ignored, whatever return you got earlier, if you are putting return in else so whatever return you got earlier that value will be finalized and this function will return, return means return this value, what does it mean everyone write in the comment, return means return this value, so this average function will return 7 value will give to c and print c will give 7, but that's not what we want, we want sum divided by len of numbers that's why we want 4.75 so we are getting 4.75 you can see here average 5671 we printed 4.75, so return statement is used to return the value of the expression back to the calling function calling function in this case is average it gets back the value which value? this value which average function has computed sum by len of numbers because it is 4.75 I hope you understood this, you can read this again and if you find
